title: Why Central Florida — The Data Behind the Choice
objectives: Interpret key economic indicators (GDP, population growth, business formation rates) that make Central Florida competitive for startups
Compare Central Florida's cost structure to Miami, NYC, Silicon Valley, and Austin using specific data points
Analyze LATAM trade corridor data (flight routes, port volume, bilingual workforce percentage) to evaluate market-bridging potential

contentBlocks.title: Central Florida by the Numbers
contentBlocks.content:
Numbers tell a story most pitch decks miss. A $500,000 seed fund buys you 10 months in Silicon Valley, but 17 months in Central Florida. That difference can mean the gap between finding product-market fit and running out of cash.

As we explored in the previous lesson, Central Florida has a collaborative culture. This lesson is the spreadsheet: the numbers to cite when building a case for your board, co-founder, or family. All data as of early 2026 unless noted.

Population and Growth

Let's break this down. Orlando-Kissimmee-Sanford metro: ~2.8 million residents. Top five fastest-growing U.S. metro over the past five years : driving consumer demand, workforce availability, and commercial real estate development.

Regional GDP

Metro Orlando GDP exceeds $150 billion annually, diversified across tourism ($75B), technology, aerospace, health care, education, and construction.

Business Formation

Florida processes 300,000+ new business filings annually via SunBiz — top three nationally. Florida LLC filing: $125. Compare: California $70 + $800 annual franchise tax, New York $200 + biennial requirements. Florida's zero state income tax applies to all pass-through business income.

contentBlocks.title: Cost Comparison — Central Florida vs. Other U.S. Entry Points
Industry Cluster Data — Where the Jobs and Revenue Are
contentBlocks.content:
Central Florida's economy is more diversified than most founders expect. Key clusters:

Simulation and Training

200+ companies, ~$6B in annual contracts. NAWCTSD is headquartered here, making Orlando the global hub for modeling, simulation, and training. Commercial applications (VR, surgical simulation) are expanding the market.

Aerospace and Defense

Space Coast (45 min from Orlando): SpaceX, Blue Origin, L3Harris, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman. 28 orbital launches in 2025 from Cape Canaveral/KSC.

Life Sciences and Health Tech

Lake Nona Medical City: Burnham Institute, Nemours, VA Medical Center, UCF College of Medicine. 90,000+ healthcare employees. ~15% YoY venture growth since 2022.

Technology and Fintech

Tech sector growing 34% faster than the national average. Major centers: Siemens Energy, Deloitte, KPMG. UCF produces 10,000+ STEM graduates annually.

Hospitality Technology

75M+ annual visitors, $75B+ tourism GDP. Disney, Universal, SeaWorld, 450+ hotel properties — B2B hospitality demand at a scale few other U.S. metros can match.

contentBlocks.title: Central Florida Industry Clusters — Key Data Points
LATAM Trade Corridor — The Data
contentBlocks.content:
For founders bridging Latin American and U.S. markets, the trade corridor data is clear:

Air Connectivity

MCO: direct flights to 30+ Latin American cities (Bogota, Sao Paulo, Mexico City, Lima, Santiago, Panama City, San Jose). ~58 million passengers in 2025, top 10 busiest U.S. airports. Terminal C (2022) added 15 international gates.

Port and Trade Volume

Florida's seaports handle $150B+ in international trade annually. Port Tampa Bay and Port Canaveral (60-90 min from Orlando) connect Latin America, the Caribbean, and the U.S. interior. Florida is the #1 U.S. state for LATAM/Caribbean trade by value.

Bilingual Workforce

30%+ of metro Orlando speaks Spanish. Bilingual accountants, attorneys, marketers, and sales professionals operate across English and Spanish daily. Hiring bilingual staff here costs significantly less than in Miami.

Growth Projections

Florida's LATAM trade grows ~4-5% annually. State population projected to reach 25 million by 2030 (from ~23M in 2025), creating an expanding customer base and workforce.

contentBlocks.title: City-by-City Comparison — What the Numbers Say
contentBlocks.content:
The cost comparison table gives you the line items. Here is the operational impact:

Central Florida vs. Miami

Office 35-40% cheaper. Housing 25-35% cheaper. Developer salaries 10-15% lower. A founder with $200K in capital gets 4-6 extra months of runway in Orlando. Miami's premium buys access to real estate, finance, and crypto ecosystems. If you are not in those sectors, it does not buy relevant access.

Central Florida vs. New York City

COL index: 98 vs. 187. A three-person team saves $180K-$250K in operating costs over 18 months. Orlando to JFK is a 2.5-hour flight. Take NYC meetings without paying NYC rent.

Central Florida vs. Silicon Valley

Developer salaries: $85K-$100K vs. $130K-$160K. A startup burning $50K/month in San Jose might burn $30K in Orlando. That is 10 extra months of runway on a $500K raise.

Central Florida vs. Austin

Closest competitor on cost (COL 108 vs. 98). Austin's advantage: tech brand recognition. Central Florida's advantage: structured international support (SBDC, CFITO, UCF BIP, Hispanic Chamber) and the LATAM trade corridor.

contentBlocks.title: Latin America Focus — Trade Data Resources
contentBlocks.content:
• CFITO upcoming trade delegations . Check cfito.org for the current calendar of delegations to Latin American markets
  • SelectFlorida . International business development resources and trade data from the state of Florida (selectflorida.org)
  • Florida Department of State, Office of International Affairs . State-level trade statistics and international business relationships (dos.fl.gov)
  • Orange County Global Investment Office . Local government resources and FDI data for international businesses locating in Orange County

contentBlocks.title: Reality Check — Numbers Don't Tell the Whole Story
contentBlocks.content:
The data favors Central Florida on cost and trade connectivity, but be honest about what the numbers do NOT capture:

  • Vehicle costs add $300-$600/month. Orlando is car-dependent. Public transit is limited. Factor this into your cost comparison. It partially offsets the housing savings vs. NYC (where you may not need a car).
  • The tech talent pool is smaller. UCF produces 10,000+ STEM grads/year, but the total tech workforce is a fraction of NYC's or Silicon Valley's. Specialized roles (ML engineers, senior DevOps) may require remote hiring.
  • VC funding is growing but concentrated elsewhere. Florida attracted about $5.5B in venture capital in 2024 — roughly 4% of the U.S. total. California captured 48%. Build traction here, but plan to pitch investors nationally.

These gaps are measurable and manageable — but include them in your decision framework.

questions.stem: A founder has $500,000 in seed funding and burns $50,000/month in Silicon Valley. Based on the cost data in this lesson, approximately how many EXTRA months of runway would she gain by relocating to Central Florida?
options: 1-2 extra months. The cost difference is marginal
3-5 extra months. Moderate savings on rent and salaries
7-10 extra months. The roughly 40% lower burn rate at ~$30,000/month extends the $500K significantly
18+ extra months . Central Florida is essentially free to operate in
questions.explanation: At $50,000/month in Silicon Valley versus approximately $30,000/month in Central Florida (based on the salary, office, and housing differentials in the cost comparison), the founder saves roughly $20,000/month. On $500,000 in funding, that extends runway from 10 months (Silicon Valley) to approximately 16-17 months (Central Florida) — roughly 7 extra months. That is the difference between finding product-market fit and running out of cash.

questions.stem: What combination of data points makes Central Florida a strong LATAM trade corridor?
options: Zero state income tax and low LLC filing fees
30+ direct flights to Latin American cities from MCO, Florida's #1 ranking in U.S.-LATAM trade by value, and a 30%+ Spanish-speaking metro population
Proximity to Disney World and Universal Studios
The highest number of Brazilian restaurants per capita in the U.S.
questions.explanation: The trade corridor argument is built on three data points: air connectivity (30+ direct LATAM routes from MCO), trade volume (Florida is the #1 U.S. state for trade with Latin America and the Caribbean by value, with $150B+ through Florida ports annually), and workforce (30%+ of metro Orlando speaks Spanish). Together, these numbers show that Central Florida is not just culturally welcoming — it is structurally positioned as a U.S.-LATAM business bridge.

contentBlocks.title: Your Location Decision Framework
contentBlocks.instructions:
Create a weighted comparison for your specific business using these steps:

  1. List your top 5 criteria for choosing a U.S. base (examples: customer proximity, cost, talent availability, flight connectivity, industry ecosystem, support programs)
  2. Weight each criterion from 1-5 based on importance to your business
  3. Score Central Florida against each criterion (1-10 scale) using the data from this lesson
  4. Score your alternative city (Miami, NYC, Silicon Valley, or Austin) against each criterion
  5. Multiply weight x score for each criterion and total them up

This exercise forces you to move beyond gut feeling and evaluate the decision with data. Bring the results to your UCF BIP conversation — it will help the team understand your priorities and advise you more effectively.

contentBlocks.title: Your Central Florida Runway Calculation
contentBlocks.prompt: Use the cost comparison table from this lesson and calculate the math for your business. Estimate your monthly burn rate in Central Florida (office/coworking + 1-2 salaries + housing + vehicle). Now estimate the same burn rate in your alternative city (Miami, NYC, Silicon Valley). How many extra months of runway does Central Florida give you? Write down the specific numbers: not "it's cheaper," but "I save approximately $X/month, which gives me Y extra months." That is the argument that convinces co-founders, investors, and family members.

contentBlocks.title: Key Takeaway
contentBlocks.content: The data makes the case: Central Florida's cost of living index (98) is nearly half of New York's (187) and well below Silicon Valley's (172). Developer salaries run $85K-$100K versus $130K-$160K in San Jose. Florida processes 300,000+ new business filings annually with a $125 LLC fee and zero state income tax. The LATAM trade corridor is backed by 30+ direct flight routes, $150B+ in annual Florida port trade, and a 30%+ Spanish-speaking metro workforce. For international founders, these numbers add up to a clear advantage: more runway, lower hiring costs, and built-in trade infrastructure connecting you to both U.S. and Latin American markets.
